channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A$AP Rocky's friend-turned-accuser to testify at LA shooting trial
item.description: A$AP Rocky 's accuser, former friend and the key witness at his trial is set to take the stand Tuesday afternoon. Rocky, the rapper and partner of Rihanna, has pleaded not guilty to two felony counts of assault with a semiautomatic firearm for allegedly firing at the friend who goes by A$AP Relli.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Why February's Supplemental Security Income checks are coming early
item.description: If you receive Supplemental Security Income from the Social Security Administration, you know the last two payments have been a bit wonky.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KREX 5

item.title: Firings, freezes and layoffs: A look at Trump's moves against federal employees and programs
item.description: CHAPIN, S.C. (AP) — Changeover among federal government agencies is normal when a new administration comes to Washington, with presidents regularly dismissing appointees selected by predecessors from opposing political parties.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A GOP lawsuit to limit people in the US illegally from the census count will be harmful, voters say
item.description: Voters in California and Texas affiliated with a Democratic group want to intervene in a lawsuit filed by four Republican state attorneys general.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from the first Trump White House briefing
item.description: President Trump’s press secretary Karoline Leavitt held her first White House briefing on Tuesday, fielding questions from reporters for just over 45 minutes.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Evacuated residents wait hours for a chance to return to burned LA neighborhoods
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Residents anxious to see what had become of their properties after the Palisades Fire ignited on Jan. 7 waited in their cars for hours Tuesday to return to their neighborhoods after officials lifted the last remaining evacuation orders.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard investigation exposes financial mismanagement
item.description: "The residents of Dolton have suffered needlessly because of the financial mismanagement of this current mayor," former Chicago Mayor Lori Lightfoot said.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, NewsNation

item.title: Subway shove survivor recalls how he cheated death after madman pushed him onto tracks
item.description: Joseph Lynskey, who was pushed in front of a New York City subway, but miraculously survived, shared the moment his life flashed before his eyes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oklahoma proposal requires parents to provide immigration status when enrolling students
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Parents enrolling children in Oklahoma public schools will be required to provide proof of their child’s U.S. citizenship or legal immigration status under a proposed rule approved Tuesday by the State Board of Education.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan Democratic Sen. Gary Peters will not run for reelection, opening a key Senate seat in 2026
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Sen. Gary Peters, who led the Democrats’ Senate campaign efforts the past two election cycles, has announced he will not seek a third term in 2026, creating a highly contested battleground seat expected to be coveted by both major political parties.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Karoline Leavitt, the youngest White House press secretary, makes her debut in the briefing room
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt made her debut in the briefing room on Tuesday and promised to speak frequently with the news media and open up the briefing room to podcasters and social media influencers.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 Tampa Bay area restaurants make list of most romantic in the US, according to OpenTable
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Valentine's Day is just around the corner and finding the perfect restaurant to take the one you love can always be challenging.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Video: Truck smashes through front of Alabama gas station
item.description: Surveillance footage from the gas station shows a white truck smashing into the front of the store and hitting the front counter.
item.dc:creator: Richard Everett, NewsNation

item.title: These are the most romantic restaurants in the US, according to OpenTable
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Valentine's Day is just around the corner and finding the perfect restaurant to take the one you love can always be challenging.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican-led states call on Costco to drop DEI
item.description: “Costco should 'do the right thing' by following the law and repealing its DEI policies,” the letter concludes.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Workers save kidnapped girl at Dothan hotel, police say
item.description: A Florida man is behind bars after police say he sexually assaulted and kidnapped a girl at an Alabama hotel.
item.dc:creator: Richard Everett, NewsNation

item.title: American Airlines worker struck, killed by vehicle at Charlotte Airport
item.description: "We are devastated by the accident involving a team member at Charlotte International Airport (CLT)," a spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, NewsNation

item.title: Weather forces Blue Origin to scrub launch of short space trip
item.description: The payloads were to experience at least two minutes of lunar gravity forces, a first for New Shepard.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Coca-Cola launches new Orange Cream flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 3.8 magnitude earthquake in Maine felt in Massachusetts, Rhode Island
item.description: A Ware, Massachusetts, resident said she was home and her whole house was shaking for about 10 seconds from what she at first believed was an 18-wheeler coming down Route 9.
item.dc:creator: Emma McCorkindale, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A fact sheet for Monday’s executive order refers to transgender Americans as “trans-identifying,” a technical term used by some to delegitimize transgender identities.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: Person seen firing gun into air amid crowd of Philadelphia Eagles fans; police investigating
item.description: Video of the alleged incident, shared to X late on Sunday, appeared to show a large crowd celebrating the Eagles' win when a person raised the gun into the air.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Is cell service getting worse? One study suggests that it is
item.description: The study, from J.D. Power, showed an increase in reported wireless network problems between the first and the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's move to freeze Biden-approved funding draws howls from Democrats
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over President Trump's move to freeze funding approved in two of former President Biden's signature laws. Trump issued an executive order pausing the disbursement of funds appropriated through the Inflation Reduction Act and the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Southern California rain helps firefighters but creates risk of flooding and toxic ash runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rain fell on parts of Southern California on Sunday and the scattered showers were expected to continue overnight, boosting the risk of toxic ash runoff in areas scorched by Los Angeles-area wildfires. Flood watches were in effect through 4 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Qatar says a deal is reached to release an Israeli hostage and allow Palestinians into northern Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Qatar announced early Monday that an agreement has been reached to release an Israeli civilian hostage and allow Palestinians to return to northern Gaza, easing the first major crisis of the fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Researchers studying whether medical cannabis could help late-stage dementia patients
item.description: As research continues, those involved say this would be a game changer, especially for patients with very limited options.
item.dc:creator: Josh Shortt, KRON4

item.title: 4 tips for launching a side hustle at any age
item.description: Either start your own business or join an existing side-hustle platform that matches people with opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, ABC 4

item.title: Former NBA star marks two years of his cannabis shop in St. Louis
item.description: There was celebration and appreciation in downtown St. Louis Friday as former NBA star Al Harrington marked the two-year anniversary of Viola Cannabis Dispensary.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Harris, ABC 4

item.title: Ozempic and similar weight loss drugs may lower risk of 42 health conditions, but also pose risks
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration’s approval of Ozempic in 2021 for weight loss treatment ushered in a new era for the class of drugs called glucagon-like peptide-1 agonists, or GLP-1.
item.dc:creator: Ziyad Al-Aly Clinical Epidemiologist, Washington University in St. Louis, Fox 5

item.title: Purdue Pharma and owners to pay $7.4 billion in settlement of lawsuits over the toll of OxyContin
item.description: Members of the family who own OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma, and the company itself, agreed to pay up to $7.4 billion in a new settlement to lawsuits over the toll of the powerful prescription painkiller, the attorneys general from several states announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4
